method_atom: Monte Carlo and molecular-dynamics simulation of a many-body system of excluded-volume particles confined to a narrow slab between two parallel structureless boundaries, computing precise spatially-averaged thermodynamic and transport quantities. The analysis shows that strong local layering (inhomogeneity normal to the boundaries) leaves most spatially-averaged properties nearly unchanged, yielding a simple analytic relation between the average density under confinement and the unconfined value at equal activity, except in a dense, tightly-confined regime. There, geometric incommensurability between the confinement width and the natural integer packing of particle layers produces coupled in-phase oscillatory deviations in an averaged thermodynamic quantity and an averaged kinetic/transport quantity as the gap width is varied.
object_atom: The equilibrium hard-sphere fluid confined between two smooth hard walls, characterized by its density, excess entropy, and self-diffusivity as functions of activity and wall-to-wall gap size.
brief_reason: What carries across is the confined-excluded-volume-particle machinery and its packing-frustration/commensurability-driven paired thermodynamic-kinetic oscillations; the domain-specific part is the hard-sphere fluid between hard walls, which is inseparably the object these averaged simulations act on.
